input.command:
rm -rf test_outputs && mkdir test_outputs && cd test_outputs && cat > example1_mig.json << 'EOF'
{
  "version": 1,
  "description": "Initial schema setup",
  "operations": [
    {
      "type": "create_table",
      "table": "users",
      "columns": [
        {"name": "id", "type": "INTEGER", "primary_key": true, "auto_increment": true},
        {"name": "email", "type": "TEXT", "not_null": true, "unique": true},
        {"name": "name", "type": "TEXT", "not_null": true},
        {"name": "created_at", "type": "TIMESTAMP", "default": "CURRENT_TIMESTAMP"}
      ]
    }
  ]
}
EOF

cat > example4_mig.json << 'EOF'
{
  "version": 4,
  "description": "Remove user_id from users",
  "operations": [
    {
      "type": "drop_column",
      "table": "users",
      "column": "age"
    }
  ]
}
EOF

# Example 1: Create users table
echo "=== Example 1: Create users table ==="
python ../migration_tool.py migrate example1_mig.json app.db

# Add some test data
sqlite3 app.db "INSERT INTO users (id, email, name, created_at) VALUES (1, 'john@example.com', 'John Doe', datetime('now'))"
sqlite3 app.db "INSERT INTO users (id, email, name, created_at) VALUES (2, 'jane@example.com', 'Jane Smith', datetime('now'))"

# Example 2: Add age column
echo ""
echo "=== Example 2: Add age column ==="
cat > example2_mig.json << 'EOF'
{
  "version": 2,
  "description": "Add age column to users",
  "operations": [
    {
      "type": "add_column",
      "table": "users",
      "column": {
        "name": "age",
        "type": "INTEGER",
        "not_null": false,
        "default": "0"
      }
    }
  ]
}
EOF
python ../migration_tool.py migrate example2_mig.json app.db

# Populate age column
sqlite3 app.db "UPDATE users SET age = 25 WHERE id = 1"
sqlite3 app.db "UPDATE users SET age = 30 WHERE id = 2"

echo ""
echo "=== Data after adding age column ==="
sqlite3 app.db "SELECT id, email, name, age FROM users"

# Example 3: Multiple operations
echo ""
echo "=== Example 3: Multiple operations ==="
cat > example3_mig.json << 'EOF'
{
  "version": 3,
  "description": "Create posts table",
  "operations": [
    {
      "type": "create_table",
      "table": "posts",
      "columns": [
        {"name": "id", "type": "INTEGER", "primary_key": true, "auto_increment": true},
        {"name": "title", "type": "TEXT", "not_null": true},
        {"name": "content", "type": "TEXT", "not_null": false},
        {"name": "created_at", "type": "TIMESTAMP", "default": "CURRENT_TIMESTAMP"}
      ]
    }
  ]
}
EOF
python ../migration_tool.py migrate example3_mig.json app.db

# Example 4: Drop column
echo ""
echo "=== Example 4: Drop column (and verify data preserved) ==="
python ../migration_tool.py migrate example4_mig.json app.db

echo ""
echo "=== Data after dropping age column ==="
sqlite3 app.db "SELECT id, email, name FROM users"

echo ""
echo "=== All migrations applied ==="
sqlite3 app.db "SELECT version, description, applied_at FROM _migrations ORDER BY version"

rm -rf test_outputs && mkdir test_outputs && cd test_outputs && cat > example1_mig.json << 'EOF'
{
  "version": 1,
  "description": "Initial schema setup",
  "operations": [
    {
      "type": "create_table",
      "table": "users",
      "columns": [
        {"name": "id", "type": "INTEGER", "primary_key": true, "auto_increment": true},
        {"name": "email", "type": "TEXT", "not_null": true, "unique": true},
        {"name": "name", "type": "TEXT", "not_null": true},
        {"name": "created_at", "type": "TIMESTAMP", "default": "CURRENT_TIMESTAMP"}
      ]
    }
  ]
}
EOF

# Example 1: Create users table
echo "=== Example 1: Create users table ==="
python ../migration_tool.py migrate example1_mig.json app.db

# Add some test data using Python
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import sqlite3
conn = sqlite3.connect('app.db')
cursor = conn.cursor()
cursor.execute("INSERT INTO users (id, email, name, created_at) VALUES (1, 'john@example.com', 'John Doe', datetime('now'))")
cursor.execute("INSERT INTO users (id, email, name, created_at) VALUES (2, 'jane@example.com', 'Jane Smith', datetime('now'))")
conn.commit()
conn.close()
PYEOF

# Example 2: Add age column
echo ""
echo "=== Example 2: Add age column ==="
cat > example2_mig.json << 'EOF'
{
  "version": 2,
  "description": "Add age column to users",
  "operations": [
    {
      "type": "add_column",
      "table": "users",
      "column": {
        "name": "age",
        "type": "INTEGER",
        "not_null": false,
        "default": "0"
      }
    }
  ]
}
EOF
python ../migration_tool.py migrate example2_mig.json app.db

# Populate age column
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import sqlite3
conn = sqlite3.connect('app.db')
cursor = conn.cursor()
cursor.execute("UPDATE users SET age = 25 WHERE id = 1")
cursor.execute("UPDATE users SET age = 30 WHERE id = 2")
conn.commit()
cursor.execute("SELECT id, email, name, age FROM users")
print("Data after adding age column:")
for row in cursor.fetchall():
    print(f"  {row}")
conn.close()
PYEOF

# Example 3: Multiple operations
echo ""
echo "=== Example 3: Create posts table ==="
cat > example3_mig.json << 'EOF'
{
  "version": 3,
  "description": "Create posts table",
  "operations": [
    {
      "type": "create_table",
      "table": "posts",
      "columns": [
        {"name": "id", "type": "INTEGER", "primary_key": true, "auto_increment": true},
        {"name": "title", "type": "TEXT", "not_null": true},
        {"name": "content", "type": "TEXT", "not_null": false},
        {"name": "created_at", "type": "TIMESTAMP", "default": "CURRENT_TIMESTAMP"}
      ]
    }
  ]
}
EOF
python ../migration_tool.py migrate example3_mig.json app.db

# Example 4: Drop column
echo ""
echo "=== Example 4: Drop age column ==="
cat > example4_mig.json << 'EOF'
{
  "version": 4,
  "description": "Remove age column from users",
  "operations": [
    {
      "type": "drop_column",
      "table": "users",
      "column": "age"
    }
  ]
}
EOF
python ../migration_tool.py migrate example4_mig.json app.db

# Verify data preserved after drop
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import sqlite3
conn = sqlite3.connect('app.db')
cursor = conn.cursor()
cursor.execute("SELECT id, email, name FROM users")
print("Data after dropping age column (data preserved):")
for row in cursor.fetchall():
    print(f"  {row}")
cursor.execute("SELECT version, description, applied_at FROM _migrations ORDER BY version")
print("\nAll migrations applied:")
for row in cursor.fetchall():
    print(f"  Version: {row[0]}, Description: {row[1]}, Applied: {row[2]}")
conn.close()
PYEOF
